# PlayCounter

Last updated: 2026-07-12
Current desktop version at publication: 1.0.6
Canonical website: https://playcounter.app/

## Short description

PlayCounter is a free, MIT-licensed game playtime tracker for Windows. It watches running processes, matches recognized executable filenames to games, and records play sessions locally. Users keep launching games through Steam, Epic Games, GOG, Game Pass, desktop shortcuts, standalone executables, or other normal methods; PlayCounter is not a launcher.

## Core behavior

- PlayCounter can start with Windows and run in the system tray.
- It notices running applications and automatically times recognized games.
- It shows the current session, per-game playtime totals and session counts, and recent session history.
- It does not import installed game libraries or historical hours from launcher accounts.
- Its record begins with sessions observed while PlayCounter is running. Older or missed time can be added manually.
- Unknown executables can be added as local custom games.
- Ambiguous or generic executable names can require a one-time local choice.
- A shared emulator process may be tracked as the emulator rather than as each individual ROM.

## Launcher independence

PlayCounter does not require Steam, Epic, GOG, EA, Ubisoft, Battle.net, Xbox, or other launcher accounts. It tracks the Windows process that actually runs, so recognized games can be timed regardless of how they were started.

## Community matching

If PlayCounter does not recognize a running executable, a user can create a local custom game or submit an executable-to-game match for review. A submitted match works locally for that user. Only an approved community match participates in automatic matching for future users. Conflicting or ambiguous matches produce a choice instead of silently creating a global one-to-one mapping.

## Privacy and networking

- Playtime, session history, settings, and local choices are stored on the user's PC.
- Automatic matching requires a request to the PlayCounter API. On Windows, the executable filename is sent; the full executable path is not sent.
- No PlayCounter account is required.
- Feedback and community match submissions are sent only when the user chooses those actions.
- Update checks and downloads use GitHub services.
- The desktop client and API source code are public for verification.

Privacy notice: https://playcounter.app/datenschutz.html

## Availability and license

- Windows: available now
- macOS: not currently distributed
- Linux: not currently distributed
- Price: free
- License: MIT

## Official links

- Website and Windows download: https://playcounter.app/
- Track playtime outside Steam and official launchers: https://playcounter.app/track-playtime-outside-steam/
- PlayCounter compared with Playnite: https://playcounter.app/playcounter-vs-playnite/
- Automatic detection and matching methodology: https://playcounter.app/how-automatic-game-detection-works/
- Supported-game behavior and limitations: https://playcounter.app/supported-games/
- Source: https://github.com/zntr1/PlayCounter
- Latest release, checksums, and release notes: https://github.com/zntr1/PlayCounter/releases/latest
- Privacy notice: https://playcounter.app/datenschutz.html
- Discord: https://discord.gg/t2nG3jaEEY
